title: Read baz marketing site content (@general subagent)

data.text:

1. Read /home/uzer/baz/content/ directory listing
2. Read any services, case studies, about, process, and portfolio content files
3. Read /home/uzer/baz/app/page.tsx or similar main page files
4. Read /home/uzer/baz/brand/ for any brand guidelines
5. Return the full text content of all marketing copy, services, case studies, testimonials, and any other content that should appear on the marketing page.

Be very thorough - read all content modules and page components.
Let me explore the directory structure first

// @ts-nocheck
"use client";
/**
 * StickyCta — fixed bottom-bar mobile call-to-action.
 *
 * Pattern from the 2026 agency-site research: 41% of agency pages bury
 * the primary CTA below the mobile fold. Digital Applied's 2026 study of
 * 2,000 landing pages found sticky-bottom CTAs lift conversion +11%.
 *
 * Behavior:
 *   - Fixed to bottom, full width, visible only on mobile (md:hidden).
 *   - Single primary button → booking URL (from lib/site.ts).
 *   - Auto-hides when an element with id="hero-cta" scrolls into view
 *     (so we never show two CTAs at the same time on mobile).
 *   - z-50 to sit above most page content; we deliberately don't go higher
 *     than the cookie banner (which is z-[60] in the layout).
 *
 * Dependency: Hero.tsx wraps its primary CTA in an element with id="hero-cta".
 * If the hero CTA is rendered without that ID, this component stays visible
 * permanently on mobile (still functional, just slightly redundant).

1: # Voice — Copywriting, Tone & Messaging
2: 
3: BAZ writes like a magazine, not a brochure. Every word must earn its place.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Brand Voice
8: 
9: ### Core Attributes
10: 
11: | Attribute | Description | Example |
12: |-----------|------------|---------|
13: | **Confident** | We state what we believe, then prove it. | "We make brands unignorable." |
14: | **Specific** | Numbers over adjectives. | "240+ brands" not "many clients" |
15: | **Editorial** | Magazine-quality writing. | Short sentences. Period. |
16: | **Honest** | No bullshit. Constraints are stated. | "Senior team, no juniors." |
17: | **Opinionated** | The agency has a viewpoint. | "Brands are verbs, not nouns." |
18: 
19: ### Anti-Voice (What We Never Do)
20: 
21: | Anti-Pattern | Example | Why |
22: |-------------|---------|-----|
23: | Corporate jargon | "leveraging synergies" | Vacuous |
24: | Superlatives without proof | "world-class", "best-in-class" | Unsubstantiated |
25: | Exclamation marks | "We're so excited!!!" | Juvenile |
26: | Emoji in professional copy | "🚀 Let's go!" | Undermines authority |
27: | Passive voice | "Growth is delivered by BAZ" | Weak |
28: | Hedging language | "We might be able to help" | Lacks confidence |
29: 
30: ---
31: 
32: ## 2. Grammar & Style
33: 
34: ### Rules
35: 
36: 1. **Oxford comma** — "brands, growth, and revenue"
37: 2. **Em dash** — Use `—` (not hyphen) for parenthetical statements
38: 3. **Sentence case** for all headings — "Powering your path to growth" (not "Powering Your Path To Growth")
39: 4. **Period** at the end of body copy, **no period** on headlines or CTAs
40: 5. **One space** after periods, never two
41: 6. **Numerals** for all numbers — "5 services" not "five services"
42: 7. **No ampersands** in body copy — use "and" (except in logo: "Brand & Buzz")
43: 8. **Abbreviations** — Spell out on first use, abbreviate after (SEO, CRM, CMO)
44: 
45: ### Punctuation
46: 
47: | Mark | Rule | Example |
48: |------|------|---------|
49: | Period | Body copy only | "We make brands unignorable." |
50: | Em dash | Parentheticals | "Senior team — no juniors — means faster shipping." |
51: | No period | Headlines, CTAs | "Get Started" |
52: | Colon | Lists, labels | "Services:" |
53: | Bullet | List items | Use `●` for eyebrows, `—` for list items |
54: 
55: ---
56: 
57: ## 3. Headline Framework
58: 
59: BAZ headlines follow a consistent structure:
60: 
61: ### Hero Headlines (Fraunces 300, 64–96px)
62: Short. Punchy. Period-optional.
63: 
64: - "Brand & Buzz."
65: - "We make brands unignorable."
66: - "From 0 to 100M views."
67: 
68: ### Section Headlines (Fraunces 400, 36–48px)
69: Declarative. Specific. No questions.
70: 
71: - "240+ brands trust BAZ"
72: - "Senior team, no juniors"
73: - "Speed is a feature"
74: 
75: ### Subheadings (Inter 400, 20px)
76: Supportive. Expanding. Never repeating the headline.
77: 
78: - Headline: "Brand & Buzz."
79: - Sub: "We make brands unignorable. From 0 to 100M views — we've done it."
80: 
81: ---
82: 
83: ## 4. Eyebrows
84: 
85: Every section starts with an eyebrow in JetBrains Mono, 11px, uppercase, tracking 0.18em, violet accent color, preceded by a `●` bullet.
86: 
87: ```
88: ● Brand & Identity
89: ● Performance Marketing
90: ● 240+ brands
91: ● Case study
92: ```
93: 
94: ### Eyebrow Rules
95: - Always uppercase
96: - Always preceded by `●` with a space
97: - Maximum 3 words ideal, 5 words maximum
98: - Always in accent color (violet)
99: - Never a full sentence — it's a label, not a headline
100: 
101: ---
102: 
103: ## 5. CTA Copy
104: 
105: ### Button Text Rules
106: - **Action-oriented** — Start with a verb
107: - **First person** — "Get my proposal" not "Get your proposal"
108: - **Specific** — "See our work" not "Learn more"
109: - **No generic text** — Never "Submit", "Click here", "Read more"
110: 
111: | Context | ✅ Good | ❌ Bad |
112: |---------|---------|--------|
113: | Primary CTA | "Start your project" | "Submit" |
114: | Secondary CTA | "See our work" | "Learn more" |
115: | Newsletter | "Get the briefing" | "Subscribe" |
116: | Contact | "Talk to us" | "Contact us" |
117: | Download | "Download the report" | "Download" |
118: | Pricing | "See pricing" | "Learn more" |
119: 
120: ### CTA Arrow
121: Primary CTAs may include a trailing arrow: "Start your project →"
122: 
123: ---
124: 
125: ## 6. Website Copy Structure
126: 
127: ### Homepage Architecture
128: 
129: ```
130: HERO
131:   Eyebrow: ● Brand & Identity
132:   Headline: Brand & Buzz.
133:   Sub: We make brands unignorable.
134:   CTAs: Start your project →  |  See our work
135: 
136: TRUST STRIP
137:   Logos or metrics: "240+ brands · $2B+ revenue · 98% retention · 12 years"
138: 
139: SERVICES
140:   Eyebrow: ● What we do
141:   Headline: Six pillars of growth
142:   Grid: 6 service cards with eyebrow + headline + description
143: 
144: CASE STUDIES
145:   Eyebrow: ● Our work
146:   Headline: Results, not promises
147:   Cards: 3 featured case studies
148: 
149: PROCESS
150:   Eyebrow: ● How we work
151:   Headline: Five phases, zero fluff
152:   Steps: Discover → Define → Design → Develop → Scale
153: 
154: CTA
155:   Eyebrow: ● Ready?
156:   Headline: Let's make your brand unignorable
157:   CTA: Start your project →
158: ```
159: 
160: ---
161: 
162: ## 7. Email Copy
163: 
164: ### Subject Lines
165: - 6–8 words maximum
166: - No ALL CAPS
167: - No emojis
168: - Specific > vague
169: 
170: | ✅ Good | ❌ Bad |
171: |---------|--------|
172: | "Your Q3 growth report" | "CHECK THIS OUT!!!" |
173: | "BAZ proposal for Acme" | "Following up" |
174: | "3 insights from our call" | "Quick question" |
175: 
176: ### Email Body
177: - Short paragraphs (2–3 sentences max)
178: - One CTA per email
179: - Sign off: "— The BAZ team" (no name)
180: 
181: ---
182: 
183: ## 8. Social Media Copy
184: 
185: ### Platform Tone Adjustments
186: 
187: | Platform | Tone | Length | CTA Style |
188: |----------|------|--------|-----------|
189: | **LinkedIn** | Professional, data-driven | 150–300 chars | "Read the full analysis →" |
190: | **X/Twitter** | Sharp, opinionated | 280 chars max | Link in bio |
191: | **Instagram** | Visual-first, editorial | 125 chars + hashtags | "Link in bio" |
192: | **YouTube** | Educational, authoritative | Title: 50 chars | "Watch next →" |
193: 
194: ### Hashtag Rules
195: - Maximum 3 hashtags per post
196: - Always lowercase
197: - Always branded: #bazagency #brandandbuzz #unignorable
198: - Never: #blessed #grateful #hustle
199: 
200: ---
201: 
202: ## 9. Marketing Language
203: 
204: ### The Five Brand Beliefs (Use as Section Eyebrows)
205: 
206: 1. **Brands are verbs, not nouns.** — A brand is something a company *does*, not something it *has*.
207: 2. **Distribution is design.** — The best creative in the world dies if nobody sees it.
208: 3. **Senior team, no juniors.** — The people who pitch are the people who ship.
209: 4. **Speed is a feature.** — Markets don't wait. We ship in weeks, not quarters.
210: 5. **Measure what matters.** — Followers are vanity. Revenue is sanity.
211: 
212: ### Power Phrases (Use in Marketing)
213: 
214: - "Powering your path to growth"
215: - "We make brands unignorable"
216: - "From 0 to 100M views — we've done it"
217: - "Senior team, no juniors, no bullshit"
218: - "Brands, buzz, and bottom-line growth"
219: 
220: ### Prohibited Phrases
221: 
222: | ❌ Never | ✅ Instead |
223: |----------|-----------|
224: | "World-class" | "240+ brands served" |
225: | "Best-in-class" | "98% client retention" |
226: | "Cutting-edge" | "AI-powered workflows" |
227: | "Synergy" | "Growth" |
228: | "Holistic" | "End-to-end" |
229: | "Revolutionize" | "Transform" |
230: | "Disrupt" | "Change" |

(End of file - total 230 lines)

1: # Visual Design System — Philosophy, Layout & Guardrails
2: 
3: This document defines the visual principles, spatial systems, elevation models, and behavioral rules for BAZ Marketing Ventures Agency. All designers and developers must implement these specifications to guarantee brand consistency.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## 1. Core Design Principles
8: 
9: ### 1.1 Editorial Luxury Through Restraint
10: BAZ's visual identity channels the authority of premium editorial publications — think *Monocle*, *Cereal*, *Kinfolk* — not the flash of consumer tech. Premium execution is achieved through:
11: - Absolute typographic clarity
12: - Vast margins of negative space
13: - Deliberate contrast between organic handwritten forms and rigid geometric grids
14: - A restrained color palette where violet is the singular accent
15: 
16: ### 1.2 Algorithmic Consistency
17: The BlackSwan Design System ensures no arbitrary values. Every color, every space, every radius is computed from three seed variables. This eliminates "designer drift" — if two elements look slightly different, one of them is wrong.
18: 
19: ### 1.3 Structural Functionalism
20: Aesthetic form follows corporate function. Every interaction must feel fast, deliberate, and premium. Decorative elements that don't serve information hierarchy are removed.
21: 
22: ### 1.4 Violet as Accent, Never Foundation
23: The violet accent `hsl(270, 85%, 72%)` is a premium signature — it commands attention precisely because it's used sparingly. It appears on:
24: - Primary CTAs
25: - Active navigation states
26: - Focus rings
27: - Data highlights
28: - Brand mark accents
29: 
30: It must **never** be used as:
31: - Large background fills
32: - Body text color
33: - Card backgrounds
34: - Section backgrounds (except `ink`-tone hero sections with violet as a highlight)
35: 
36: ---
37: 
38: ## 2. Layout & Negative Space
39: 
40: ### 2.1 Macro Whitespace
41: Maintain a minimum of 120px (3rem) separation between major content sections on large viewports. Sections breathe. They never crowd.
42: 
43: ### 2.2 Micro Whitespace
44: Form fields, interactive components, and list items must use generous interior padding per the 8pt rhythm system defined in `spacing.md`.
45: 
46: ### 2.3 The Law of Spatial Isolation
47: The BAZ wordmark requires a clear zone equal to 35% of its total rendered height on all four sides. No text, borders, or graphic elements may encroach on this zone.
48: 
49: ### 2.4 Section Tones
50: Three reusable section backgrounds via `<Section tone="...">`:
51: 
52: | Tone | Background | Heading Color | Purpose |
53: |------|-----------|--------------|---------|
54: | `paper` | `#f5f1ea` | `text-ink-900` | Default. Most sections |
55: | `white` | `#ffffff` | `text-ink-900` | Visual breathing between paper sections |
56: | `ink` | `#0e0e0e` | `text-paper` | KPI bands, CTAs, callouts |
57: 
58: ---
59: 
60: ## 3. Elevation, Radius & Layering
61: 
62: ### 3.1 Fibonacci Radius System
63: BAZ uses a Fibonacci-based radius scale — not arbitrary 4px/8px/12px increments. This creates organic, Da Vinci-inspired curvature that feels premium and intentional:
64: 
65: | Token | Value | Use |
66: |-------|-------|-----|
67: | `--radius-fib3` | 3px | Badges, tags, micro-overlays |
68: | `--radius-fib5` | 5px | Inputs, small buttons, toggles |
69: | `--radius-fib8` | 8px | Standard buttons, dropdowns |
70: | `--radius-fib13` | 13px | Cards, modals, panels |
71: | `--radius-fib21` | 21px | Hero containers, feature blocks |
72: | `--radius-fib34` | 34px | Large overlay panels |
73: | `--radius-fib55` | 55px | Full-width sections |
74: | `--radius-fib89` | 89px | Hero blobs, decorative elements |
75: | `--radius-full` | 9999px | Pills, avatars, circular elements |
76: 
77: ### 3.2 Elevation & Shadow Tokens
78: BAZ uses a flat-yet-layered depth model inspired by Linear and Vercel. Shadows are subtle, crisp, and never diffuse:
79: 
80: | Level | Token | Use | Shadow |
81: |-------|-------|-----|--------|
82: | 0 | `--shadow-none` | Flat canvas | none |
83: | 1 | `--shadow-xs` | Resting cards | `0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.03)` |
84: | 2 | `--shadow-sm` | Hovering cards | `0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,.06), 0 1px 2px -1px rgba(0,0,0,.06)` |
85: | 3 | `--shadow-md` | Elevated panels | `0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,.07), 0 2px 4px -2px rgba(0,0,0,.05)` |
86: | 4 | `--shadow-lg` | Floating dropdowns | `0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0,0,0,.08), 0 4px 6px -4px rgba(0,0,0,.04)` |
87: | 5 | `--shadow-xl` | Modals, overlays | `0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,.10), 0 8px 10px -6px rgba(0,0,0,.04)` |
88: | 6 | `--shadow-2xl` | Hero emphasis | `0 25px 50px -12px rgba(0,0,0,.16)` |
89: 
90: On dark (`ink`) sections, shadows strengthen:
91: - `--shadow-md` becomes `0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,.4)`
92: - `--shadow-xl` becomes `0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,.5)`
93: 
94: ### 3.3 Surface Layers (Material 3 inspired)
95: BAZ implements a 5-tier surface system:
96: 
97: | Tier | CSS Variable | Light | Dark | Use |
98: |------|-------------|-------|------|-----|
99: | 0 | `--bg` | `#f5f1ea` (paper) | `hsl(270, 8%, 6%)` | Page background |
100: | 1 | `--bg-muted` | `#faf7f1` | `hsl(270, 6%, 9%)` | Muted sections |
101: | 2 | `--bg-subtle` | `#f0ebe0` | `hsl(270, 5%, 14%)` | Cards on paper |
102: | 3 | `--bg-emphasis` | `#e0d8cc` | `hsl(270, 2%, 22%)` | Elevated cards |
103: | 4 | `--surface-inverse` | `#0e0e0e` | `#f5f1ea` | Inverted sections |
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 4. Component Architecture
108: 
109: ### 4.1 BlackSwan Components (Hand-Rolled)
110: BAZ uses the BlackSwan Design System for components — **never shadcn/ui**. All components are built from CSS custom properties that cascade from the three seed variables. Components include:
111: 
112: - **Button** — 6 variants × 5 sizes + icon, block, connected group
113: - **Input** — Text, textarea, select, checkbox, radio, toggle
114: - **Card** — 5 variants (default, flat, bordered, ghost, highlight)
115: - **Paper** — Long-form article surface, 3 variants
116: - **Document** — Invoice/contract/letter template
117: - **Alert** — 4 severity levels with icon support
118: - **Badge** — 6 colors, 3 sizes, dot indicator
119: - **Toast** — Notification toasts with enter/exit animation
120: - **Modal** — Dialog overlay with 4 sizes
121: - **Table** — Data table with striped variant
122: - **Avatar** — 6 sizes, status dots, stack
123: - **Skeleton** — Shimmer loading placeholders
124: 
125: ### 4.2 Component Principles
126: 1. **Zero runtime JS for styling** — CSS custom properties only
127: 2. **Zero build step** — just import the CSS
128: 3. **Zero dependencies** — no npm packages for styling
129: 4. **The cascade is the algorithm**
130: 
131: ---
132: 
133: ## 5. Brand Guardrails
134: 
135: ### ✅ Do
136: - Use `ink` (#0e0e0e) and `paper` (#f5f1ea) as foundational canvases
137: - Pair the BAZ wordmark with structured Fraunces/Inter typography
138: - Maintain 120px+ macro whitespace between sections
139: - Use violet `hsl(270, 85%, 72%)` as the singular accent for CTAs and highlights
140: - Apply Fibonacci radii (3, 5, 8, 13, 21…) for organic curvature
141: - Use subtle 1px violet rules as section dividers
142: - Use the magnetic CTA pattern for hero buttons
143: 
144: ### ❌ Don't
145: - Use violet as a background fill for large areas
146: - Apply drop shadows, outer glows, or heavy reflections to the wordmark
147: - Mix gold (#f9a01f) and red (#ff3b2f) as simultaneous accents
148: - Use shadcn/ui components — always use BlackSwan
149: - Use arbitrary 4px/8px/12px radius values — stick to Fibonacci
150: - Use Playfair Display (use Fraunces instead)
151: - Use Tailwind v4 syntax (use v3 only)
152: 
153: ---
154: 
155: ## 6. UI Examples
156: 
157: ### 6.1 Landing Page Masthead
158: ```
159: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
160: │  [BAZ mark]         NAV ITEMS         [CTA →]  │
161: │                                                  │
162: │                                                  │
163: │        Brand & Buzz.                            │
164: │        We make brands                            │
165: │        unignorable.                              │
166: │                                                  │
167: │           [Get Started]  [Our Work]              │
168: │                                                  │
169: │    ● 240+ brands  ● $2B+ revenue  ● 12 years   │
170: │                                                  │
171: │         ═════════════════════════                │
172: │              (divider line)                      │
173: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
174: ```
175: 
176: ### 6.2 Stat Grid Pattern
177: ```
178: ┌──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┐
179: │  240+    │  $2B+    │  98%     │  12      │
180: │  brands  │  revenue │  retain  │  years   │
181: └──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┘
182: ```
183: Background: `paper` with `ink-100` 1px dividers between cells.
184: 
185: ### 6.3 Dark Section (Ink Tone)
186: ```
187: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
188: │  ████████  INK BACKGROUND (#0e0e0e)  ████████  │
189: │                                                  │
190: │     "Brands are verbs, not nouns."              │
191: │                  — BAZ                           │
192: │                                                  │
193: │     [Start Your Project →]                       │
194: │                                                  │
195: └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
196: ```
197: White/violet text on ink. No gradients.

(End of file - total 197 lines)
</content>

- Vast margins of negative space
- Deliberate contrast between organic handwritten forms and rigid geometric grids
- A restrained color palette where violet is the singular accent

### 1.2 Algorithmic Consistency
The BlackSwan Design System ensures no arbitrary values. Every color, every space, every radius is computed from three seed variables. This eliminates "designer drift" — if two elements look slightly different, one of them is wrong.

### 1.3 Structural Functionalism
Aesthetic form follows corporate function. Every interaction must feel fast, deliberate, and premium. Decorative elements that don't serve information hierarchy are removed.

## BRAND GUIDELINES (`brand/`)
**Voice (`voice.md`):** Magazine not brochure. Confident, Specific, Editorial, Honest, Opinionated. No jargon, no superlatives without proof, no exclamation marks, no emoji, no passive/hedging. Five brand beliefs: Brands are verbs not nouns · Distribution is design · Senior team no juniors · Speed is a feature · Measure what matters.

**Design (`design.md`):** Editorial luxury through restraint (Monocle/Cereal/Kinfolk). Violet `hsl(270,85%,72%)` is the singular accent (never a background/body fill). Three section tones: paper `#f5f1ea`, white `#ffffff`, ink `#0e0e0e`. 120px macro whitespace. Fibonacci radius scale (3/5/8/13/21…). BlackSwan components, never shadcn/ui.
